summary: Vibrant set with flowing keys, stellar Wharf Rat → The Other One
blurb: Set two delivers a high‑energy run featuring a smoking Wharf Rat → The Other One sequence and a slow‑sweet Ship Of Fools. Brent’s sustained keyboard lines shine throughout, while Jerry’s solos are on fire. Overall the show is a tight, upbeat performance with strong second‑set rockers.
review: On May 31, 1980 at Minneapolis’ Metropolitan Sports Center the Dead turned in an upbeat, tightly knit set that still feels fresh today. The first half rolled along with classic road‑trip fare – Alabama Getaway into The Promised Land and a crisp “Deal” to close – but it’s the second set that truly dazzles: a fluid “Feel Like A Stranger,” a slow‑sweet, Garcia‑on‑it rendition of “Ship Of Fools,” and an inspired segue from “Wharf Rat” straight into a raging “The Other One.” Brent Mydland’s keyboards glide with a flowing sustain rarely heard since the Pigpen days, adding a lush backdrop to the band’s tight rhythm section. Jerry’s solos are described as “on fire,” especially during the Wharf‑Rat→Other‑One stretch, and even Bob Weir holds his own, sounding as good as Jerry on this night. The post‑drums flow – “I Need A Miracle” into “Bertha” and a bright “Sugar Magnolia” – rounds out a set that never loses momentum, capped by a double encore of “U.S. Blues” and “Brokedown Palace.” For fans seeking the definitive listening experience, the Miller soundboard capture (gd1980-05-31.set2.sbd.miller.91763.sbeok.flac16) offers crystal‑clear board tone with only a brief dropout during the Wharf → Other One transition.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are marked positive, using strong praise such as “vibrant,” “high‑energy,” and “smoking” to describe the performance; multiple sources highlight the same standout moments (Wharf Rat → The Other One, Brent’s flowing keys, Jerry on fire), indicating consistent enthusiastic sentiment across analyses.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, balanced tone; only a brief dropout during the Wharf → Other One transition, making it the clearest representation of the show’s performance.

key_highlights: Brent's flowing, sustained keyboard lines that recall the Pigpen era
Smoky, high‑energy Wharf Rat → The Other One sequence
Jerry delivering a slow‑sweet Ship Of Fools and fire‑blazing solos later in the set
A memorable split double encore with U.S. Blues and Brokedown Palace

band_performance.Jerry: On fire with solid, creative solos; delivers a slow‑sweet version of Ship Of Fools and drives the high‑energy moments.
band_performance.Bob: Sounds as good as Jerry that night, holding his own throughout the set.
band_performance.Brent: Flows with sustained, lyrical keyboard lines not heard since the Tom Constanten/Pigpen era, adding depth to every song.
